title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 22 - A Quick Look at Some Expansion Heroes
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DI-Oxm-J0W0

---

Daniel (@dungeondive) takes a quick tour through the expansion heroes available for Shadows of Brimstone, showcasing around 24 heroes across his collection. Each hero gets a brief overview of their unique mechanics and the proxy miniatures Daniel uses when the original resin quality falls short.

Highlights include the Orphan, whose side-quest mechanic lets them discover their parentage and dual-class into another hero type; the Dark Stone Shaman with tribal magic, a summoning circle tile, and the ability to shapeshift into a bear or field mouse; the Frontier Doc who doubles as healer and specimen-collecting scientist; and the Jargono Native, a primitive hunter who can't use guns, books, or tech items.

The Wandering Samurai comes with a cautionary tale about overpaying on eBay before Kickstarter exclusives hit retail. The Cowboy remains mysteriously unexplored despite being purchased. The Sumo — inspired by E. Honda from Street Fighter 2 — brings belly smash and multi-hand slap attacks. The Trederian Veteran arrives from another world with faction-specific starting items, and the Prospector rounds things out as a crotchety pickaxe-wielding miner with great health and sanity but terrible speed.

Daniel notes that single hero packs are finally hitting retail at around $20-30 each, though he wishes they'd bundle sets of four at a lower price point.
